title: Ethereum can quantum-proof accounts for just 7 cents, says Ethereum's Kohaku lead
summary: The SPHINCS- proposal aims to reduce the cost of post-quantum signature verification on Ethereum while the network works toward a longer-term solution.

title: Humanity Protocol’s $36M hack tied to suspected North Korean hackers: Quantstamp
summary: A fake Bithumb email used in the $36 million Humanity Protocol hack points to the involvement of North Korean threat actors, according to Quantstamp.

title: Lazard Undercuts Rival Centerview in Battle for Venezuela Deal
summary: Investment bank Lazard Inc. is making a late bid to dislodge rival Centerview Partners as Venezuela’s financial adviser, offering to oversee one of the largest-ever sovereign debt restructurings for a sharply lower fee.

title: IDC, Vision Near Deal to Save Century-Old African Sugar Mills
summary: Tycoon-backed Vision Group is in talks with South Africa’s Industrial Development Corp. to take an equity stake in sugar refiner Tongaat Hulett in exchange for additional funding, Vision Group shareholder and consortium member Robert Gumede said in an interview.

title: Pokémon Card Sales Are Surging on Crypto Platforms—Just Don't Call It Gambling
summary: Tokenized Pokémon card sales have skyrocketed over the past year, fueled by a wave of speculation and so-called gacha machines.

title: US Government Orders Anthropic to Pull Claude Fable, Mythos AI Models
summary: Anthropic pushed back against what it described as an overreach, saying the vulnerability cited is already widespread across the industry.

title: Trump-backed World Liberty Financial to fund UFC fighter bonuses in USD1 stablecoin at White House event
summary: The deal puts USD1 branding inside the Octagon at an event held on the White House South Lawn, on President Trump's 80th birthday.

summary: Venice founder Erik Voorhees and the official Morpheus account seized on the shutdown, casting it as proof of "permissionless" AI's use case.

title: How Israel’s ‘trampoline’ economy has adapted to war
summary: After two decades of growth the country has proven to be surprisingly resilient to war and energy shocks
